budget: Add a generic `pi` owner namespace to the workplan control root so pi-driven open-weights seats (GLM, Kimi, MiniMax via the pi CLI) can claim and own cards under their real identity instead of presenting a false codex/claude identity, which the append-only ledger honesty rules forbid (META-30; user decision 2026-07-24: generic `pi`, NOT per-provider namespaces — provider identity stays visible via the session segment of the pi:<host>:<session> owner string). Exactly three enforcement points plus one documentation point: (1) the claim owner regex in parseClaimOwner (scripts/workplan.mjs:192, currently ^(codex|claude):([^:]+):([^:]+)$) — extend to accept pi, and update the rejection message at :194 to name pi; (2) the allowedOwnerNamespaces whitelist in validateDefinition (scripts/workplan.mjs:1177 ["codex","claude"].includes(ns), error at :1183) — include pi and update the error text; (3) the remote gateway qualify-time namespace set in _validate_definition_contract (development/remote_workplan/gateway.py:235, currently {codex, claude, dev-orchestrator}) — add pi; the gateway's claim/reassign/revalidate owner_namespace_denied checks (:501-503, :713-714, :738-739) are generic per-card subset checks and need no enum change, only test coverage. (4) Add a `pi` lane label to the AGENTS.md:38 lane-label tuple and the :27 seat framing, disambiguating from the existing 'Pi/NeuralWatt GLM-5.2' reviewer-seat usage in AGENTS.md:47-79. Regression tests required in scripts/workplan.test.mjs (following existing conventions: a piOwner(session) helper alongside codexOwner/claudeOwner at :135-141; models at :2543-2585 disallowed-namespace claim, :2962-2989 and :3409-3445 claim-next eligibility) and in tests/development/test_remote_workplan_gateway.py (model at :319-337; also add the previously missing qualify-time negative case for an out-of-set namespace at gateway.py:236), proving: pi:<host>:<session> owners are accepted at claim/start/submit and by the gateway where a card's allowedOwnerNamespaces includes pi; pi owners are still rejected where a card's frozen namespaces exclude pi; unknown namespaces remain rejected everywhere; existing codex/claude behavior is unchanged. Cards already Ready keep their frozen namespaces. Scope is exactly the five reserved paths; no receipt hash-chaining work (that is TASK-20260724-019), no new CLI subcommands, no provider-specific namespaces, no changes to the coordinator: owner regexes (workplan.mjs:169,207; gateway.py:493-494,696-697), and no changes to claim-next ordering or any other workplan semantics.
stopCondition: Stop and fail closed on: any edit outside the five reserved paths (scripts/workplan.mjs, scripts/workplan.test.mjs, development/remote_workplan/gateway.py, tests/development/test_remote_workplan_gateway.py, AGENTS.md); any weakening of existing owner validation (host-match check, nonempty-session check, rejection of unknown namespaces, frozen per-card allowedOwnerNamespaces enforcement, coordinator: owner rules); any change to receipt or ledger semantics; any relaxation that lets a card whose frozen allowedOwnerNamespaces excludes pi be claimed, reassigned to, or revalidated for a pi owner; any new dependency; a failing acceptance command; or an unresolved P0/P1 review finding.
evaluatorAuthority: The implementing seat may not self-approve. Independent provider-diverse reviewers examine the frozen base..head diff read-only per AGENTS.md and return P0-P3 findings with file:line evidence, preserved verbatim under .review-store/. Any P0/P1 requires a new commit and a fresh frozen-diff review; after any fix round, re-freeze the card to the head actually reviewed before submit. Behavioral verification must drive a real pi CLI seat against a disposable control root (throwaway git repo, not the live board): ready a test card with allowedOwnerNamespaces ["pi"], have the pi seat run claim, start, and submit under its own pi:<host>:<session> identity, confirm the receipts record that identity verbatim, and confirm the negative case that claiming a claude-only card fails with the namespace rejection; the seat transcript is captured under .review-store/. A distinct coordinator actor runs integrate and accept. This card grants no promotion, deployment, evaluator-write, or credential authority.
nextCheckpoint: A pi:<host>:<session> owner can claim, start, and submit a card whose frozen allowedOwnerNamespaces includes pi, at the CLI and through the remote gateway, with receipts recording the pi identity verbatim; a pi owner is still rejected on cards whose frozen namespaces exclude pi and unknown namespaces are still rejected everywhere; all existing suites stay green; and a real pi seat has demonstrated the full claim-start-submit lifecycle plus the negative case against a disposable control root with the transcript preserved under .review-store/.
